title: Reasons for determination — July 3, 2026
summary: Canadian companies exporting steel products to the U.S. will face new tariffs starting July 2026 after a trade dispute ruling, increasing their costs and potentially reducing competitiveness in American markets.

title: Reasons for determination — June 24, 2026
summary: Canada's International Trade Tribunal rejected a dumping complaint against imported steel, meaning Canadian businesses can continue importing this product without facing new tariffs or trade barriers.

title: Determination — June 18, 2026
summary: Canadian importers of steel products must now pay higher tariffs after the Canadian International Trade Tribunal confirmed that dumped Chinese and Indian steel is harming domestic producers.

title: Determination — June 9, 2026
summary: Canadian manufacturers of seamless pipes and tubes face a 15-25% tariff on imports from Japan and South Korea, protecting domestic producers from cheaper foreign competition starting immediately.

title: Reasons for determination — June 1, 2026
summary: Canada's International Trade Tribunal ruled that imports of certain steel products no longer injure domestic producers, so tariffs on these items will be removed, lowering costs for Canadian manufacturers using steel.

title: Determination — May 15, 2026
summary: Canadian manufacturers exporting softwood lumber to the U.S. will face higher tariffs starting immediately, making their products more expensive for American buyers and potentially reducing export volumes.

title: Notice of commencement of preliminary injury inquiry — April 21, 2026
summary: Canada is investigating whether imports of a specific product are injuring domestic producers, which could lead to import tariffs or quotas within months.
